county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Osborne County formed 1867, organized 1871. County seat is Osborne. Named for Vincent B. Osborne, killed in Civil War. North-central Kansas. Solomon River valley. Geographic center of the contiguous US (near Lebanon in Smith County) is nearby.
county_metadata.research_tips: Osborne County Register of Deeds in Osborne holds land records from the 1870s. The Osborne County Historical Society and Museum has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. The geographic center of the contiguous United States is near Lebanon in Smith County but the area draws researchers to Osborne County as well. Scandinavian and German immigrant communities left church records. Probate Court records from the 1870s. Osborne County Farmer newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Osborne County records. The Solomon River valley was the primary settlement area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Osborne County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Osborne

source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1867; deed books are substantially complete

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Ottawa County formed 1860. County seat is Minneapolis (not Minnesota). Named for the Ottawa people. Solomon River valley. Swedish and Mennonite settlers. Central Kansas agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Ottawa County Register of Deeds in Minneapolis holds land records from the 1860s. The Ottawa County Historical Society and Museum in Minneapolis has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Swedish settlers in the Lindsborg area (shared with McPherson County) left Lutheran church records. Probate Court records from the 1860s. Minneapolis newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Ottawa County records. The Solomon River valley was the primary settlement corridor. Mennonite communities also settled in the county; check Bethel College archives. District Court naturalization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Ottawa County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Minneapolis

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Pawnee County formed 1867, organized 1872. County seat is Larned. Named for the Pawnee people. Fort Larned (1859-1878) was a major Santa Fe Trail military post. Fort Larned National Historic Site. Central Kansas agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Pawnee County Register of Deeds in Larned holds land records from the 1870s. Fort Larned (1859-1878) was a major military post on the Santa Fe Trail -- extensive military records at NARA. Fort Larned National Historic Site has related documentation. The Pawnee County Historical Society in Larned has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. Church records from Methodist and Catholic congregations from the 1870s. Probate Court records from the 1870s. Larned newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Pawnee County records. The Santa Fe Trail crossed through the county; check KSHS for trail-era records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Pawnee County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Larned

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Phillips County formed 1867, organized 1872. County seat is Phillipsburg. Named for Colonel William A. Phillips, Civil War officer. North-central Kansas bordering Nebraska. Kirwin Reservoir. Czech and German settlers.
county_metadata.research_tips: Phillips County Register of Deeds in Phillipsburg holds land records from the 1870s. The Phillips County Historical Society and Museum has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Scandinavian and German immigrant communities left church records from the 1870s-1880s. Probate Court records from the 1870s. Phillipsburg newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Phillips County records. The county borders Nebraska; cross-border family research may be needed. Kirwin Reservoir construction affected some communities. The county had Czech settlers whose records may be in Catholic parish registers.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Phillips County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Phillipsburg

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Pottawatomie County formed 1857 from unorganized territory. County seat is Westmoreland. Named for the Pottawatomie people. John Brown's Pottawatomie Massacre (1856) occurred here. Tuttle Creek Lake construction displaced several communities. Wamego is the largest city.
county_metadata.research_tips: Pottawatomie County Register of Deeds in Westmoreland holds land records from 1857. The county was named for the Pottawatomie people -- tribal records at NARA Kansas City. John Brown's Pottawatomie Massacre (1856) occurred here; territorial-era records at KSHS. The Pottawatomie County Historical Society in Westmoreland has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. German and Scandinavian settlers left church records from the 1860s. Probate Court records from 1857. Westmoreland and Wamego newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Pottawatomie County records. Tuttle Creek Lake construction displaced several communities including the original town of Randolph; check KSHS for relocation records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Pottawatomie County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Westmoreland

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Pratt County formed 1879 from Stafford County. County seat is Pratt. Named for Lieutenant Caleb Pratt, killed in Civil War. South-central Kansas wheat farming county. Kansas Department of Wildlife and Parks headquarters. German-Russian and Mennonite settlers.
county_metadata.research_tips: Pratt County Register of Deeds in Pratt holds land records from 1879. Formed from Stafford County. The Pratt County Historical Society and Museum has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. German-Russian and Mennonite settlers left church records from the 1880s. Probate Court records from 1879. Pratt newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Pratt County records. The county was an important wheat farming area. District Court naturalization records. The Kansas state fish hatchery near Pratt means Kansas Department of Wildlife records are headquartered here.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Pratt County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Pratt

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records begin from practical settlement in the 1879s-1889s

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Rawlins County formed 1873, organized 1881. County seat is Atwood. Named for General John A. Rawlins, US Army. Northwestern Kansas county bordering Nebraska. Late settlement through homesteading. Dust Bowl effects. Sparsely populated.
county_metadata.research_tips: Rawlins County Register of Deeds in Atwood holds land records from the 1880s. A northwestern Kansas county with late settlement. The Rawlins County Historical Society and Museum in Atwood has local family files. BLM GLO land patents are essential for tracing homesteaders. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer congregations are sparse. Atwood newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Dust Bowl effects caused outmigration in the 1930s. The county borders Nebraska; cross-border family research may be needed.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Rawlins County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Atwood

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Reno County formed 1867, organized 1872. County seat is Hutchinson. Named for General Jesse L. Reno, killed in Civil War. Hutchinson is the 'Salt City' with extensive salt mining. Kansas State Fair since 1913. Mennonite communities. One of the larger Kansas counties.
county_metadata.research_tips: Reno County Register of Deeds in Hutchinson holds land records from 1867. Hutchinson is the 'Salt City' -- salt mining records document workers. The Reno County Historical Society and Museum in Hutchinson has excellent local collections. The Kansas State Fair has been held in Hutchinson since 1913. BLM GLO land patents. German-Russian and Mennonite settlers left extensive church records from the 1870s -- Buhler, Inman, and Pretty Prairie congregations. Probate Court records from 1867. Hutchinson News newspaper at KSHS and Newspapers.com. FamilySearch has microfilmed Reno County records. The Kansas Underground Salt Museum documents the mining industry. District Court naturalization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Reno County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Hutchinson

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Republic County formed 1860. County seat is Belleville. Named to honor the republic form of government. Northern Kansas county bordering Nebraska. Republican River valley. Czech, Scandinavian, and German settlers. Lovewell Reservoir.
county_metadata.research_tips: Republic County Register of Deeds in Belleville holds land records from the 1860s. The Republic County Historical Society and Museum in Belleville has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Scandinavian, Czech, and German settlers left church records from the 1860s-1880s. Probate Court records from the 1860s. Belleville Telescope newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Republic County records. The Republican River valley was the primary settlement area. The county borders Nebraska; cross-border family research may be needed. Lovewell Reservoir construction affected some communities.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Rice County formed 1867, organized 1871. County seat is Lyons. Named for General Samuel A. Rice, killed in Civil War. Coronado-Quivira historical area. Santa Fe Trail corridor. German-Russian, Mennonite, and Swedish settlers. Sterling College.
county_metadata.research_tips: Rice County Register of Deeds in Lyons holds land records from 1871. The Rice County Historical Society and Coronado-Quivira Museum in Lyons has local collections documenting both Spanish exploration era and later settlement. BLM GLO land patents. German-Russian, Mennonite, and Swedish settlers left church records from the 1870s. Probate Court records from 1871. Lyons newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Rice County records. Sterling College archives hold local church records. The county was on the Santa Fe Trail. District Court naturalization records at the courthouse.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Rice County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Lyons

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Rice County; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters

source_profiles.source_name: Rice County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1870-1950 for Rice County; Kansas Territory census 1855 and 1859 may include Rice County entries at KSHS; state census 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905, 1915, 1925 at KSHS

source_profiles.source_name: Rice County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (homestead, preemption, timber culture, cash entry) for Rice County; Kansas is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Rice County Mennonite and German-Russian Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / Bethel College Mennonite Library and Archives, North Newton / KSHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mennonite church records (General Conference, Mennonite Brethren, Church of God in Christ Mennonite) including baptisms, marriages, burials, and membership rolls for Rice County; Bethel College Mennonite Library and Archives holds church records, immigration documents, and passenger lists from Russia/Ukraine

source_profiles.source_name: Rice County Marriage Records -- Probate Court / KSHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Rice County Probate Court, Lyons / Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records for Rice County from 1867; Probate Court issued marriage licenses in Kansas; KSHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Kansas marriages; state vital records registration began 1911
